## Wave Description

This wave wires up the MMM Simple PR Admin Model bypass in all CI ceremony gates. Product-fix
PRs declaring `requires_iaa: false` or `requires_ecap: false` in `.admin/pr.json` now skip the
corresponding IAA/ECAP ceremony gate jobs. Governance-control PRs (`governance-change`,
`agent-contract-change` types, or governance-control paths) are forced to `requires_iaa/ecap: true`
by `validate-simple-pr-admin.sh` CHECK 8, preserving full ceremony requirements.

## Ripple/Cross-Agent Assessment

| Agent / System | Change Scope Assessed | Impact Conclusion |
|---|---|---|
| foreman-v2-agent | governance canon files, CI gate scripts, preflight workflow | NO IMPACT — governance tooling wave; no app code, schema, or public API changed |
| independent-assurance-agent | CI gate scripts (iaa-final-assurance-gate.sh, ecap-admin-ceremony-gate.sh) | NO IMPACT — bypass only fires when `.admin/pr.json` declares `requires_iaa/ecap: false`; IAA enforcement unchanged for governed PRs |
| execution-ceremony-admin-agent | ecap-admin-ceremony-gate.sh, preflight-evidence-gate.yml | NO IMPACT — ECAP bypass requires `requires_ecap: false`; governance-change PRs remain fully enforced |
| all other agents | no public API, schema, or contract files changed | NO IMPACT — downstream effect: none |

**Downstream ripple conclusion**: NO IMPACT — governance/ceremony artifacts and CI tooling only, no downstream effect on any app, schema, API, or agent contract.
